programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Sewing: Open Studio
programs.description: Students who have already completed a sewing course at the Clark Centre can join this open studio session for self-directed work. Use our sewing machines to complete your ongoing work or start new patterns. Bring your own fabric, thread and desired tools as there are limited shared items (ex. measuring tape, bobbins, tailors chalk, etc.) Materials are not provided. An instructor will be present to assist where needed.Please note: The start date listed in the brochure is July 29. The start date for this course is actually Tuesday, July 28.

programs.name: Sewing: Open Studio

Bring your own fabric, thread and desired tools as there are limited shared items (ex. measuring tape, bobbins, tailors chalk, etc.) Materials are not provided. An instructor will be present to assist where needed.

programs.name: Silkscreen Printing Workshop
programs.description: For participants who don't want to know every technical detail about screen printing but have always wanted to try it and have fun doing it! Participants will learn how to silkscreen using the many screen designs provided (words, trees, birds, etc.). Some paper and fabric will be provided but bring your own materials to make prints more personal (t-shirts, napkins, tea towels, journals, wood panels, etc.). Please wear clothes that are comfortable and can get dirty, no long hanging sleeves. A material fee of $15 (cash only) payable to instructor for inks.

programs.name: Soccer
programs.description: Get moving with non-stop soccer play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in soccer offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Soccer: House League
programs.description: Be part of a team! Our soccer house league activity is structured to teach you the basic skills and rules of the game, and then put them into action through recreational level competitive play. As you learn and develop soccer fundamentals, you will also learn the importance of teamwork and respectful play. Game times are built into scheduled classes, no additional days/times are required.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. programs.name: Toronto East York Inclusive Request
programs.description: Our inclusive services are designed for participants who may require additional assistance to participate in recreation activities. Inclusive staff assist participants by providing modifications in program instruction, movement, equipment and communication. They also promote and encourage socialization amongst peers. Assignments of Inclusive staff are based on individual application and availability.As this is only a request, participants who waitlist for this activity will be contacted by Adapted and Inclusive Recreation Services for follow-up.

programs.name: Toronto Women's Softball: House League B Division
programs.description: The B Division is recommended for recreational players with some playing experience. Participants may register individually or as part of a team. All players must email marcel.vieira@toronto.ca with a brief summary of their playing experience.Softball games may take place at either of the two diamonds. Full schedules will be shared with each team prior to the start of the season.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
